nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 金绣眼鸟
nodes.summary: 金绣眼鸟（Golden white-eye，学名 Cleptornis marchei），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，主要分布于森林，常见栖息地包括灌丛，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、小种群风险。

nodes.name: 森林
nodes.summary: 森林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联402种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、Blue-throated piping guan、White-throated piping guan等402种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is common in native forests, particularly limestone forests, but also occurs in open shrubland and suburban areas

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: It is common in native forests, particularly limestone forests, but also occurs in open shrubland and suburban areas

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: All three species are insectivorous and were theorised to have declined due to habitat loss

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Nevertheless, the species remained relatively abundant, and the current world population is estimated at around 71,997 birds. The species is nonetheless evaluated as Endangered by the IUCN because the population is expected to undergo a rapid decline if the brown tree snake becomes established on Saipan. The primary threat to this species is the brown tree snake, a native of Australia, New Guinea and the Solomon Islands, which eliminated all 12 landbird species on nearby Guam after being accidentally introduced. The snake has not yet become established on Saipan, one of the two islands that compose the range of the golden white-eye, and which holds the largest population of the species. The isolation of Aguijan makes the introduction of brown tree snakes there unlikely, but the small population there is vulnerable as the island is only 718 ha (1774 acres) in size and a direct hit by a supertyphoon could wipe them out. Efforts are under way to breed the species in captivity{{cite news|first=Jeffrey |last=Bonner |title=Reptilian invasion: Saving Pacific island birds from snakes |year=2008 |url=http://www.stltoday.com/stltoday/lifestyle/stories.nsf/travel/story/D121C8812E3E31D1862574B9006CCB82?OpenDocument |work=St
